article_meta.title: Once upon a time, school students would submit an essay, and teachers would mark it

reasoning: The model avoided quoting sources and flagged some genuine author-level framing (e.g., "innovative ways"). However, it overreaches on one Slant example (neutral adoption stats) and misses clear biased wording in the lede ("interrogate them," "put them on the spot") and a likely unattributed claim ("Early results show...").

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged items come from the author’s prose; no quoted or clearly attributed source language was flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: One flagged Slant instance (the Talis adoption statistic) is weak/arguable as bias; the others are defensible.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed clear author-level loaded wording like "interrogate them"/"put them on the spot" and a probable Omission of Source Attribution in "Early results show it has saved time...".
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels are mostly appropriate (Word Choice, Slant), though one Slant label is debatable for a neutral statistic.
